2026-04-08T02:01:40.6415285Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:41.8513943Z **Summary of gaps found in PR #57134 by @radhgupta:**
2026-04-08T02:01:41.8515601Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:43.0300972Z - **CHANGELOG.md** describes the old CLI architecture (`generate`/`migrate` commands) — completely replaced by the new MCP server; needs full rewrite
2026-04-08T02:01:43.0308293Z - **README "Getting started"** lacks MCP server startup (`--mcp-server`) and registration instructions for VS Code / Claude Desktop
2026-04-08T02:01:44.2612313Z - **README tool count inconsistency** — prose says "18 tools", table shows 16, actual implementation has 19; `discover_project`, `generated_snapshot`, and `add_code_gen_suppress` are missing from the table
2026-04-08T02:01:44.3645026Z - **Missing "Authenticate the client" section** (standard README template requirement; answer: no auth needed)

This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. Key additions:\n- `--mcp-server` mode serving 19 auto-discovered tools over stdio\n- `DeterministicFixRegistry` with 27 error-pattern-to-tool rules\n- 19 individual MCP tools (regex fixes, nullable annotations, code gen, finalization, etc.)\n- Skill-driven workflow via `.github/skills/sdk-migration/SKILL.md`\n- 93 new NUnit tests (243 total)\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **CHANGELOG.md describes the old CLI architecture**, not the new MCP server architecture. It says \"Added `generate` command\" and \"Added `migrate` command\" — neither of these commands exist in the refactored codebase; the entry point is now `McpServerHost.RunAsync` serving tools over stdio.\n- **README \"Getting started\" section has no MCP server invocation instructions** — it shows only `dotnet build` but not how to run the server (`dotnet run -- --mcp-server`), how to register it in VS Code / Claude Desktop `settings.json`, or how to configure it for Copilot.\n- **README \"Key concepts\" tool count is inconsistent**: says \"18 individual tools\" in prose but the table lists only 16; the `src/Mcp/Tools/` directory contains 19 tool files. Three tools are absent from the table: `DiscoverProjectTool`, `GeneratedSnapshotTool`, and `AddCodeGenSuppressTool`.\n- **README has no \"Authenticate the client\" section** (or explicit note that no auth is required), which is required by the standard README template.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### File 1: `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/CHANGELOG.md` — **Edit**\n\nReplace the entire body of the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` entry with accurate content reflecting the MCP refactor:\n\n```markdown\n# Release History\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Initial release of `Azure.GeneratorAgent` as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for automating Azure SDK code generation and migration workflows.\n- Added `--mcp-server` mode that exposes 19 auto-discovered deterministic fix tools over stdio transport.\n- Added `DeterministicFixRegistry` with 27 rules that map build error codes and message patterns to specific tool invocations, covering field renames, type-pattern replacements, missing/obsolete using directives, nullable annotation fixes, and method call replacements.\n- Added MCP tools: `regex_replacement`, `add_using_directive`, `remove_using_directive`, `nullable_annotation_fix`, `batch_fix`, `build_and_classify`, `classify_errors`, `run_code_generation`, `validate_tsp_config`, `commit_iteration`, `pregen_cleanup`, `migrate_test_samples`, `finalize_migration`, `run_tests`, `rename_codegen_type`, `fetch_to_fromlro`, `discover_project`, `generated_snapshot`, `add_code_gen_suppress`.\n- Each tool supports both MCP (JSON over stdio) and in-process invocation for testing.\n- Skill-driven workflow: the [`sdk-migration`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/sdk-migration/SKILL.md) skill doc drives the full migration pipeline by calling MCP tools directly.\n\n### Breaking Changes\n\n- Initial release, no breaking changes.\n\n### Bugs Fixed\n\n- Initial release, no bugs fixed.\n\n### Other Changes\n\n- Added 243 NUnit tests covering all MCP tools, the deterministic fix registry, build output parsing, and error classification.\n```\n\n#### File 2: `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` — **Edit**\n\n**A. Fix tool count and add missing tools to the MCP Tools table.**\n\nReplace:\n```\nEach tool supports both MCP (JSON) and in-process invocation.\n```\nwith:\n```\nEach tool supports both MCP (JSON) and in-process invocation.\n```\n(no change to sentence, but fix number in prose: change \"18 individual tools\" → \"19 individual tools\")\n\nAdd the three missing rows to the MCP Tools table:\n\n```markdown\n| `discover_project` | Discover the `.csproj` and output path for a given service directory |\n| `generated_snapshot` | Capture a snapshot of generated files before migration for diff comparison |\n| `add_code_gen_suppress` | Add `[CodeGenSuppress]` attributes to suppress specific generated members |\n```\n\n**B. Add MCP server startup and registration instructions to the \"Getting started\" section.**\n\nAfter the existing `dotnet build` block, add a new subsection:\n\n```markdown\n### Run as MCP server\n\nStart the server over stdio (used by Copilot and other MCP clients):\n\n```bash\ndotnet run --project sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent -- --mcp-server\n```\n\n#### Register in VS Code (Copilot)\n\nAdd to `.vscode/mcp.json` (or your user-level `settings.json` under `\"mcp\"` → `\"servers\"`):\n\n```json\n{\n  \"servers\": {\n    \"generator-agent\": {\n      \"type\": \"stdio\",\n      \"command\": \"dotnet\",\n      \"args\": [\n        \"run\",\n        \"--project\",\n        \"${workspaceFolder}/sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent\",\n        \"--\",\n        \"--mcp-server\"\n      ]\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n#### Register in Claude Desktop\n\nAdd to `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` (macOS) or `%APPDATA%\\Claude\\claude_desktop_config.json` (Windows):\n\n```json\n{\n  \"mcpServers\": {\n    \"generator-agent\": {\n      \"command\": \"dotnet\",\n      \"args\": [\n        \"run\",\n        \"--project\",\n        \"C:\\\\git\\\\azure-sdk-for-net\\\\sdk\\\\tools\\\\Azure.GeneratorAgent\",\n        \"--\",\n        \"--mcp-server\"\n      ]\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Authenticate the client\n\nNo authentication is required. The MCP server operates entirely on the local file system and does not call any Azure service endpoints.\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nNo test-backed snippets are required for this change. The Examples section already contains illustrative shell/JSON blocks that do not need to be injected from test files.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nAfter the edits, the README must contain these sections in order. 2026-04-08T02:01:48.5398428Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5398531Z **Package:** `Azure.GeneratorAgent`
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5398680Z **Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/`
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5398823Z **Triggered by:** PR #57134 (commit `82d240a1b`) by @radhgupta
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5398827Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5398912Z ## What Changed
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5398916Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5399452Z PR #57134 refactored `Azure.GeneratorAgent` from a CLI tool with `generate`/`migrate` commands into an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server + tools + skill architecture. Key additions:
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5399629Z - `--mcp-server` mode serving 19 auto-discovered tools over stdio
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5399803Z - `DeterministicFixRegistry` with 27 error-pattern-to-tool rules
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5400045Z - 19 individual MCP tools (regex fixes, nullable annotations, code gen, finalization, etc.)
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5400218Z - Skill-driven workflow via `.github/skills/sdk-migration/SKILL.md`
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5400318Z - 93 new NUnit tests (243 total)
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5400323Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5400399Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5400403Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5401458Z - **CHANGELOG.md describes the old CLI architecture**, not the new MCP server architecture. It says "Added `generate` command" and "Added `migrate` command" — neither of these commands exist in the refactored codebase; the entry point is now `McpServerHost.RunAsync` serving tools over stdio.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5402523Z - **README "Getting started" section has no MCP server invocation instructions** — it shows only `dotnet build` but not how to run the server (`dotnet run -- --mcp-server`), how to register it in VS Code / Claude Desktop `settings.json`, or how to configure it for Copilot.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5403384Z - **README "Key concepts" tool count is inconsistent**: says "18 individual tools" in prose but the table lists only 16; the `src/Mcp/Tools/` directory contains 19 tool files. Three tools are absent from the table: `DiscoverProjectTool`, `GeneratedSnapshotTool`, and `AddCodeGenSuppressTool`.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5403793Z - **README has no "Authenticate the client" section** (or explicit note that no auth is required), which is required by the standard README template.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5403797Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5403876Z <details>
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5404100Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>

2026-04-08T02:01:48.5404869Z #### File 1: `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/CHANGELOG.md` — **Edit**
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5404874Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5405181Z Replace the entire body of the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` entry with accurate content reflecting the MCP refactor:
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5405191Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5405261Z ```markdown
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5405460Z # Release History
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5405464Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5405553Z ## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5405557Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5405634Z ### Features Added
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5405638Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5406076Z - Initial release of `Azure.GeneratorAgent` as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for automating Azure SDK code generation and migration workflows.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5406363Z - Added `--mcp-server` mode that exposes 19 auto-discovered deterministic fix tools over stdio transport.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5407199Z - Added `DeterministicFixRegistry` with 27 rules that map build error codes and message patterns to specific tool invocations, covering field renames, type-pattern replacements, missing/obsolete using directives, nullable annotation fixes, and method call replacements.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5408418Z - Added MCP tools: `regex_replacement`, `add_using_directive`, `remove_using_directive`, `nullable_annotation_fix`, `batch_fix`, `build_and_classify`, `classify_errors`, `run_code_generation`, `validate_tsp_config`, `commit_iteration`, `pregen_cleanup`, `migrate_test_samples`, `finalize_migration`, `run_tests`, `rename_codegen_type`, `fetch_to_fromlro`, `discover_project`, `generated_snapshot`, `add_code_gen_suppress`.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5408640Z - Each tool supports both MCP (JSON over stdio) and in-process invocation for testing.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5409305Z - Skill-driven workflow: the [`sdk-migration`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/sdk-migration/SKILL.md) skill doc drives the full migration pipeline by calling MCP tools directly.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5409314Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5409392Z ### Breaking Changes
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5409396Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5409496Z - Initial release, no breaking changes.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5409500Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5409572Z ### Bugs Fixed
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5409576Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5409671Z - Initial release, no bugs fixed.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5409678Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5409750Z ### Other Changes
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5409753Z 
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5410106Z - Added 243 NUnit tests covering all MCP tools, the deterministic fix registry, build output parsing, and error classification.
2026-04-08T02:01:48.5410179Z ```
